{"step_by_step_thinking": "The Transplantation of Human Organs Act specifies the qualifications for declaring brainstem death. Typically, this responsibility falls on a neurologist or a neurosurgeon, who are experts in neurological functions. The treating physician and the surgeon performing a liver transplant would not have the specialized training necessary to make this determination. The RMP (Responsible Medical Practitioner) in charge of the hospital, however, is generally authorized to declare brainstem death, as they oversee the medical care and have the necessary authority to make such a critical decision.", "answer_choice": "A"}
